coredata.dc:title: Preparation and fluorescence detection of nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots/cotton fiber composites
coredata.dc:creator: Xing, Zhenfei
Chen, Ru
Zhang, Xin
Song, Jinghui
Cao, Xinmiao
Zhao, Guangtai
Li, Ying
Sun, Zhijing
coredata.dc:description: From the perspective of environmental protection and sustainable development, fluorescent probes have been prepared using inexpensive raw materials for fluorescence detection in wastewater treatment. Nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots (N-CQDs)/cotton fiber composites were prepared by grafting N-CQDs onto the oxidized cotton fibers using a silane coupling agents. Through a series of characterizations using X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and X-ray energy dispersive spectrometry (EDS), it was found that N-CQDs were loaded onto cotton fibers. In the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composite, the fluorescence rate of carbon quantum dots is higher, which is 17.2 %, and the detection limit is 1.21 μM. The optimum conditions for fluorescence detection of azo dye Chrome black T(EBT) with pH 8 and concentration 10 μM were studied. This method for the detection of EBT has advantages such as high sensitivity, simplicity, speed, reusability, and a high potential for application in wastewater detection.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Carbon quantum dot
Cotton fiber
Fluorescence detection
Composite material

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Preparation of nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots
Preparation of nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots/cotton fiber composites

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency-matched-string: Natural Science Foundation of Liaoning Province

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: The authors thank the funding from the Natural Science Foundation of Liaoning Province (No. 20180550053) and the scientific research project (2022-FX-001) of Dalian Product Quality Inspection and Testing Research Institute Co. Ltd.
The authors thank the Natural Science Foundation of Liaoning Province (no. 20180550053 ).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A)Synthesis route of N-CQDs, (B) mechanism of possible reaction of oxidized cotton fiber with KH-560, (C) synthesis route of NKC.
(A) EDS diagram of KC and elements (C, O, and N) in KC and (B) EDS diagram of NKC and elements (C, O, and N) in NKC.
The pictures for cotton fibers,(A) daylight, (B) UV lamp, and fluorescence spectra of (C) NKC-10, (D) NKC-20, and (E) NKC-30.
(A), (C), and (E) Fluorescence quenching spectra of N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites with different concentrations of EBT added; (B), (D), and (F) the dependence of F0/F-1 on EBT concentration.

head.ce:title: Preparation and fluorescence detection of nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots/cotton fiber composites

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Textile and Material Engineering, Dalian Polytechnic University, Dalian 116034, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Textile and Material Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Textile and Material Engineering, Dalian Polytechnic University, Dalian 116034, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Dalian Product Quality Inspection and Testing Research Institute Co., Ltd, Dalian 116034, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Dalian Product Quality Inspection and Testing Research Institute Co., Ltd

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Dalian Product Quality Inspection and Testing Research Institute Co., Ltd, Dalian 116034, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: From the perspective of environmental protection and sustainable development, fluorescent probes have been prepared using inexpensive raw materials for fluorescence detection in wastewater treatment. Nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots (N-CQDs)/cotton fiber composites were prepared by grafting N-CQDs onto the oxidized cotton fibers using a silane coupling agents. Through a series of characterizations using X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and X-ray energy dispersive spectrometry (EDS), it was found that N-CQDs were loaded onto cotton fibers. In the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composite, the fluorescence rate of carbon quantum dots is higher, which is 17.2 %, and the detection limit is 1.21 μM. The optimum conditions for fluorescence detection of azo dye Chrome black T(EBT) with pH 8 and concentration 10 μM were studied. This method for the detection of EBT has advantages such as high sensitivity, simplicity, speed, reusability, and a high potential for application in wastewater detection.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The authors do not have permission to share data.

ce:section.ce:para: Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) are a new type of fluorescent nanomaterials with a size of less than 10 nm. They have many excellent properties [1], such as tunable fluorescence characteristics, high photoluminescence properties, good water solubility, bleaching performance, biocompatibility, and low toxicity [2–5]. Therefore, they are widely used in drug delivery, fluorescent probes, photovoltaic devices, and cell imaging [6–9].

Cotton fiber is an environmentally friendly material with excellent characteristics, such as softness, renewability, comfort, moisture absorption, and biodegradability [10]. As the most widely distributed natural carbohydrate, cotton fiber accounts for 38 % of the total biomass on Earth and is the main component of the carbon and energy cycle in the Earth's biosphere [11]. The structural units, i.e., the cellulose molecules, have three active hydroxyl groups with different characteristics in heterogeneous chemical reactions and can undergo reactions such as esterification, oxidation, and graft copolymerization [12].

In order to improve the reusability of carbon quantum dots, this study modified the hydroxyl group on the surface of cotton fiber to enhance its functionality, and simply and conveniently prepared nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots/cotton fiber composite material, which was first applied to the high sensitivity and rapid detection of azo dye chromium black T in wastewater, providing a possibility for the study of harmful dyes in wastewater.

Citric acid, urea, ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid (EDTA) quinine sulfate, Chrome Black T(EBT), sodium hypochlorite, ethanol, C-glycidyl ester propyl trimethoxy-silane (KH-560),acetic acid, acetone, and formic acid were purchased from Tianjin Kemei Ou Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine oxide was purchased from Anhui Kuer Biological Engineering Co., Ltd.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots
ce:section.ce:para: First, 1 g citric acid, 1 g urea and 0.2 g EDTA were weighed, and then N-CQD powder was obtained by ultrasound, microwave, centrifugation, dialysis, and freeze-drying for 24 h.

The initial step involves immersing the sample in boiling acetone for a duration of 30 min to eliminate any wax, oil, and other impurities present on the cotton surface. Subsequently, the hydroxyl groups on the cotton fiber surface are oxidized to carboxyl groups using the 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine oxide (TEMPO) oxidation method. Finally, C-glycidyl ester propyl trimethoxy-silane (KH-560) is employed as the coupling agent of choice. This results in obtaining a modified cotton fiber containing KH-560 referred to as KC [13].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots/cotton fiber composites
ce:section.ce:para: Grafted cotton fibers, KC (0.4 g), were added to 50 mL of nitrogen-doped carbon quantum dots in an aqueous solution and stirred at room temperature for 12 h. Subsequently, carbon quantum dots/cotton fiber (NKC)composites were prepared. Based on this method, 50 mL of a mixed solution (10, 20, and 30 mL N-CQDs solution) was prepared and then combined with KC to obtain NKC samples containing different concentrations of N-CQDs. They were labeled as NKC-10, NKC-20, and NKC-30, respectively [11]. Therefore, the schematic diagram of the preparation process of carbon quantum dot modified cotton fiber composite material is shown in Fig. 1 .

Fig. 2 (A) and2(B) show the X-ray energy spectra of the cotton fiber and NKC, respectively. In the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites, the gradual increase in the N-CQD content increased the ratio of C to O in the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites from 1.7 to 1.8, indicating a successful connection between the N-CQDs and cotton fibers. The N content in the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites was also higher than that in the cotton fibers, where the N content increased from 0.45 to 3.91, owing to the many N-CQDs on the surface of the cotton fiber. These results indicate that N-CQDs were loaded onto the cotton fibers through the connection made by the silane coupling agent, and N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites were successfully prepared [14].

Fig. 3 (A) and (B) show images captured under NKC fluorescent light and NKC UV light, respectively. Because the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites had no fluorescence under fluorescent lamp irradiation, it was confirmed that N-CQDs were loaded on the cotton fiber, and the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites were successfully prepared. Fig. 3(C), (D), and (E) show the fluorescence spectra of NKC-10, NKC-20, and NKC-30, respectively. A When the excitation wavelength was greater than 310 nm, the fluorescence position of the N-CQD/cotton fiber composite material shifted to blue, which could be attributed to the different luminescence sites of the CQDs [15].
Fig. 4 shows the dependence of the fluorescence intensity of the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites on EBT concentration.

The fluorescence-quenching sensitivity of the N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites to EBT was also investigated. Fig. 4 (A), (C), and (E) show the fluorescence spectra of NKC-10, NKC-20, and NKC-30, respectively, at different EBT concentrations (0–100 μM). When the concentrations of EBT were 0–50 μM, the fluorescence intensity of NKC was linearly correlated with EBT. Therefore, N-CQDs/cotton fiber composites can be used for the sensitive detection of azo dye EBT [16].

In this study, N-CQDs prepared using the microwave method were immobilized on the surface of cotton fibers to prepare N-CQDs/cotton fiber composite materials. The fluorescence detection sensitivity of chromium black T was high and could be reused, improving the reuse rate of N-CQDs. The simple, environmentally friendly, and highly reusable N-CQDs/cotton fiber composite materials prepared in this study have great potential for application in wastewater detection.

Zhenfei Xing: Data curation, Writing – original draft. Ru Chen: Conceptualization, Methodology, Writing – review & editing, Supervision, Funding acquisition. Xin Zhang: Data curation, Validation. Jinghui Song: Data curation, Validation. Xinmiao Cao: Formal analysis, Investigation. Guangtai Zhao: Data curation, Formal analysis. Ying Li: Resources, Supervision, Project administration. Zhijing Sun: Resources, Supervision, Project administration.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors thank the funding from the Natural Science Foundation of Liaoning Province (No. 20180550053) and the scientific research project (2022-FX-001) of Dalian Product Quality Inspection and Testing Research Institute Co., Ltd.
